# Implementation Plan: Model Reasoning Level Selection

**Branch**: `545-model-reasoning-selection` | **Date**: 2026-04-02 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/545-model-reasoning-selection/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command. See `.specify/templates/commands/plan.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

Thread the GitHub Copilot SDK's existing reasoning-level data (`supported_reasoning_efforts`, `default_reasoning_effort`) end-to-end — from model listing through session creation to frontend selectors — so users can choose and apply reasoning levels (low, medium, high, xhigh) per model. The approach adds two optional fields to `ModelOption`, passes a new `reasoning_effort` parameter through the completion and agent paths, expands reasoning-capable models into per-level variants in the frontend `useModels()` hook, and displays `ReasoningBadge` indicators in all model selectors. Separate fields (not composite IDs) ensure backwards compatibility.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python ≥3.12 (target 3.13) for backend; TypeScript ~6.0.2 / React 19.2.0 for frontend  
**Primary Dependencies**: FastAPI, Pydantic, GitHub Copilot SDK (`copilot` package), `agent_framework_github_copilot`; React, TanStack Query, Tailwind CSS, Lucide Icons  
**Storage**: JSON settings file (file-based, no database)  
**Testing**: pytest (backend unit tests), Vitest + React Testing Library (frontend), contract validation via `scripts/validate-contracts.sh`  
**Target Platform**: Linux server (backend), modern browsers (frontend)  
**Project Type**: Web application (frontend + backend)  
**Performance Goals**: No new performance requirements; model list endpoint latency unchanged  
**Constraints**: Backwards compatible — empty `reasoning_effort` = API default; Copilot provider only (Azure OpenAI out of scope)  
**Scale/Scope**: ~15 files modified across backend and frontend; no new services or infrastructure

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Evidence |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| **I. Specification-First** | ✅ PASS | `spec.md` created with prioritized user stories (P1-P2), GWT acceptance scenarios, and independent test criteria |
| **II. Template-Driven Workflow** | ✅ PASS | All artifacts follow canonical templates from `.specify/templates/` |
| **III. Agent-Orchestrated Execution** | ✅ PASS | Plan phase produces well-defined outputs; tasks phase will decompose into executable units |
| **IV. Test Optionality** | ✅ PASS | Tests are included per issue requirements (Phase 4). Backend and frontend tests specified. |
| **V. Simplicity and DRY** | ✅ PASS | Uses separate fields (not composite IDs); expansion logic centralized in `useModels()` hook; no premature abstraction. Single `ReasoningBadge` component reused across selectors. |

**Gate Result**: ✅ ALL PASS — Proceed to Phase 0.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/545-model-reasoning-selection/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── spec.md              # Feature specification
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
│   └── model-option-reasoning.yaml  # OpenAPI delta for ModelOption
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
solune/
├── backend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── models/
│   │   │   ├── settings.py          # ModelOption + AIPreferences (add reasoning fields)
│   │   │   └── pipeline.py          # PipelineAgentNode (reasoning_effort in config)
│   │   └── services/
│   │       ├── model_fetcher.py     # fetch_models() — extract reasoning data from SDK
│   │       ├── completion_providers.py  # complete() — pass reasoning_effort to SessionConfig
│   │       ├── agent_provider.py    # _create_copilot_agent() — inject reasoning_effort
│   │       └── workflow_orchestrator/
│   │           └── orchestrator.py  # _resolve_effective_model() → also resolve reasoning_effort
│   └── tests/
│       └── unit/
│           ├── test_model_fetcher.py        # Verify reasoning fields in model list
│           ├── test_completion_providers.py  # Verify reasoning_effort in SessionConfig
│           └── test_orchestrator.py         # Verify reasoning resolution precedence
├── frontend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── types/
│   │   │   └── index.ts            # AIModel, AIPreferences, PipelineAgentNode types
│   │   ├── services/
│   │   │   └── api.ts              # modelsApi.list() mapper
│   │   ├── hooks/
│   │   │   └── useModels.ts        # Expand reasoning variants
│   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   ├── pipeline/
│   │   │   │   ├── ModelSelector.tsx         # ReasoningBadge in ModelRow
│   │   │   │   ├── AgentNode.tsx             # Pass reasoning_effort on selection
│   │   │   │   └── PipelineModelDropdown.tsx # Reasoning variant display
│   │   │   └── settings/
│   │   │       ├── DynamicDropdown.tsx       # Reasoning variant expansion
│   │   │       └── PrimarySettings.tsx       # Save reasoning_effort separately
│   │   └── lib/
│   │       └── icons.ts             # Add Brain icon re-export
│   └── src/
│       ├── hooks/
│       │   └── useModels.test.tsx   # Test expansion logic
│       └── components/
│           └── pipeline/
│               └── ModelSelector.test.tsx  # Test ReasoningBadge rendering
└── scripts/
    ├── export-openapi.py            # Regenerate OpenAPI schema
    └── validate-contracts.sh        # Validate backend/frontend contract sync
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application (Option 2). The existing `solune/backend/` + `solune/frontend/` structure is preserved. No new directories or services are added — all changes are modifications to existing files.
